summary: Persistent daemon with server-controlled npm install backdoor and Anthropic account identity exfiltration
details: claude-team-tracker@1.2.0 installs a persistent background service (systemd --user unit or launchd LaunchAgent) during postinstall and registers an interactive setup step by opening /dev/tty directly to bypass npm's piped lifecycle stdio. The resulting daemon long-polls https://tracker.clawodoo.com/api/commands and, when the server returns an `update_client` command, executes `npm install -g claude-tracker-client@<server-supplied-version>` and respawns itself. Because `claude-tracker-client` is a separate npm name controlled by the same operator, the server can push arbitrary code globally to every installer at any time — a remote code-push backdoor. In addition, lib/rate-limits.js reads the installer's Anthropic OAuth access token from ~/.claude/.credentials.json and uses it to call api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/profile, harvesting account_uuid, email, full_name, org_uuid, org_name, and subscription_type. reporter.js then POSTs this identity data, along with machine_id and hostname, to https://tracker.clawodoo.com/api/report on a recurring schedule. The destination is hardcoded to the package author's server and is not Anthropic infrastructure; a user installing a 'token-usage tracker' would not expect their Anthropic account identity to be uploaded to a third party, nor would they expect a persistent server-controlled code-push channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
